none: Vinyl chloride emission standard : environmental impact statement.

The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed a national emission standard for vinyl chloride to address its health risks, including cancer, for people exposed to it in the air. This standard aims to significantly reduce emissions from ethylene dichloride-vinyl chloride and polyvinyl chloride plants by using the best available control technology. The goal is to protect public health by minimizing exposure risks for communities near these plants. The standard is expected to reduce emissions by about 94-95% in typical plants, although it may also lead to increased emissions of other substances like hydrogen chloride.
Vinyl chloride emission standard : environmental impact statement.

The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed a national emission standard for vinyl chloride to address its health risks, including cancer, for people exposed to it in the air. This standard aims to significantly reduce emissions from ethylene dichloride-vinyl chloride and polyvinyl chloride plants by using the best available control technology. The goal is to protect public health by minimizing exposure risks for communities near these plants. The standard is expected to reduce emissions by about 94-95% in typical plants, although it may also lead to increased emissions of other substances like hydrogen chloride.
Society of the Plastics Industry, Inc.
National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health

The proposed emission standard for vinyl chloride was initiated by the EPA in response to growing concerns about the health risks associated with exposure to this chemical, particularly its links to cancer. There was public concern regarding the safety of communities located near vinyl chloride production facilities, which prompted the need for stricter regulations. The proposal aimed to implement best available control technologies to significantly reduce emissions, thereby protecting public health.
